metadata.title: 'Foreign influence in the campaign against American AI' — the Bitcoin Policy Institute's Singham/PSL data-center thesis (documented network vs directed-campaign claim, dated)
metadata.status: The Singham funding network, the nonprofit ecosystem, the specific data-center-opposition campaigns, the Chinese/Russian state-media items, the foreign-foundation grants, and the Congressional/DOJ inquiries are individually sourced FACT (NYT 2023, IRS 990s, Delhi Police chargesheet, agency/Congressional records, the reports' own citations). The reports' overarching thesis — that these amount to a coordinated FOREIGN INFLUENCE CAMPAIGN directing US opposition to AI infrastructure — is GRADED (inferential); the $23.6B aggregate is WEAK (methodology unstated by the report itself). The publisher's pro-AI interest is flagged.
metadata.frame: A three-part series by the Bitcoin Policy Institute (BPI; author Sam Lyman) argues a China-linked foreign-influence network has amplified US opposition to AI data centers, slowing American AI buildout. Part I (May 18 2026) casts a wide net — Chinese state media (CGTN/China Daily/Global Times), Russian RT, and foreign billionaires (Swiss Hansjörg Wyss ~$700M; British Alan Parker/Oak Foundation >$750M) — plus Chinese academics (Zeng Yi, Xue Lan) testifying before a Senate (Sanders) panel Apr 29 2026. Part II (Jun 29 2026) is the 'ground game': ~$278M from Neville Roy Singham-controlled entities into six US nonprofits (2017-23) whose leadership overlaps the Party for Socialism and Liberation (PSL), which ran/joined 21 data-center-opposition campaigns across 14 states. Part III (Jun 29 2026) tallies '$23.6B in blocked/stalled AI infrastructure.' The strongest sub-claim (Singham network + CCP-aligned content) is well-documented; the causal 'directed campaign against American AI' is the report's inference, which the report itself repeatedly hedges. Attaches to China, CCP, Huawei, US_Congress, AI_Datacenters, and the influence/manufactured-division thread.

the_series: Three BPI reports by Sam Lyman (Head of Research): Part I 'Foreign Influence in the Campaign Against American AI' (2026-05-18); Part II 'Singham Ground Game' (2026-06-29); Part III 'China-Aligned Ground Game Stalled or Blocked $23.6 Billion in American AI Infrastructure' (2026-06-29). BPI is a 501(c)(3) (EIN 88-1567390). grade: fact (authorship/dates).
the_singham_network: The documented core: Neville Roy Singham — US citizen based in Shanghai, sold software firm Thoughtworks (~$785M), self-described Marxist, prior Huawei consulting, and (per NYT, 2023-08-05) worked from a Shanghai office shared with a Chinese media firm and attended a CCP propaganda workshop. The NYT traced hundreds of millions to a network mixing progressive advocacy with Chinese-government talking points; a Delhi Police chargesheet (May 2024) named the Chinese government the network's 'ultimate paymaster.' Fox News Digital's reading of IRS Form 990s (Mar 2026) put ~$278M flowing from Singham-controlled entities into six US nonprofits (2017-23): The People's Forum, ANSWER Coalition, BreakThrough News, Tricontinental Institute, Justice and Education Fund, and CodePink (co-founded by Jodie Evans, Singham's spouse). Sen. Rubio referred Singham entities to DOJ for FARA review (2023); reporting describes a DOJ grand-jury probe. grade: fact (the network, the money, the CCP-aligned content, the referrals/probe). Singham's response (2023): he is 'not a member of, work for, take orders from, or follow instructions of any political party or government.'
the_ground_game: Part II's overlap thesis: PSL (Party for Socialism and Liberation, a Marxist-Leninist group) shares leadership with the Singham-funded nonprofits — e.g., Claudia De la Cruz (People's Forum co-founder; PSL 2024 presidential nominee), Karina Garcia (Justice & Education Fund; PSL VP nominee), Ben & Brian Becker, Eugene Puryear (ex-Radio Sputnik host), Manolo De Los Santos, Medea Benjamin, Gloria La Riva. PSL and allied groups participated in ~21 data-center-opposition campaigns across 14 states (canvassing, petitions, packed council chambers, rallies; slogan 'America's Dairyland, not America's Data Land'). Documented outcomes attributed at least in part to this activity: ~10 local moratoria (e.g., Prince George's County MD, Charlotte NC, Madison/Dane County WI, DeKalb/Athens-Clarke GA, Durham/Cumberland NC, Denver CO, Box Elder UT), a permanent ban (Monterey Park CA, June 2026, 86% approval), and 4 rejections/withdrawals (Tucson/Chandler AZ; a $1B Google project in Franklin Township IN; a $12B QTS/Blackstone campus in DeForest WI). grade: fact (that PSL/these groups ran or joined these campaigns; the campaign outcomes). CAUSATION explicitly hedged BY THE REPORT: 'The PSL's role in each of these campaigns ranged from lead organizer to one member of a broader coalition.'
the_broader_claim: Part I widens the aperture beyond Singham: (a) Chinese state media (CGTN/China Daily/Global Times) ran attributed items warning US audiences that data centers are environmentally/economically harmful — while China subsidizes its own AI-datacenter energy costs (the report reads the asymmetry as strategic intent); (b) Russian RT promoted data-center bans in ME/NY/SC/OK; (c) foreign billionaires Hansjörg Wyss (Swiss, ~$700M via charitable vehicles) and Alan Parker (British, Oak Foundation >$750M) fund US advocacy groups; (d) a '230+ organization' coalition letter (Dec 8 2025) demanded moratoria, followed ~107 days later by a Sanders bill; (e) Singham-funded Tricontinental opposed US semiconductor export controls with framing echoing China's Foreign Ministry. grade: MIXED. The state-media items and the foundation grants are documented; but (i) Wyss/Parker are Swiss/British funders of broad progressive causes, so folding them into a 'foreign influence against American AI' frame is a stretch (contested); (ii) parallel messaging + shared talking points is documented, but 'coordination/direction' is inferred, not shown.
the_23_6b_figure: Part III's headline number ($23.6B blocked/stalled) is the weakest link: the report does NOT state its aggregation methodology, and names only three projects with dollar figures (Prince George's County MD $5B permitting halt; DeForest WI $12B Blackstone/QTS campus; Monterey Park CA ban, amount unspecified) — which sum to ~$17B, not $23.6B, with the remainder unexplained. grade: weak (the specific blocked projects are real; the aggregate $23.6B is an unverified construction).
the_publisher_interest: Read-words-for-intent applies to the framer too: the Bitcoin Policy Institute is a pro-Bitcoin / pro-AI-infrastructure think tank, so it has a direct interest in recasting data-center opposition as foreign-influenced rather than as legitimate local dissent. That does not make the documented facts false — the Singham network and the CCP-aligned content are corroborated well beyond BPI (NYT, IRS filings, Delhi chargesheet, Congressional letters) — but it means the SELECTION and CAUSAL FRAMING (which opposition counts, how much agency to assign the network) should be read as advocacy, and the load-bearing inferences graded independently of the publisher.
composition_and_causation_guard: The error to avoid is composition/division: treating 'documented Singham funding + CCP-aligned content + PSL participation in some campaigns' as 'the American anti-data-center movement is a Chinese operation.' The reports themselves disclaim the maximal read — Part II: 'This report does not assert that members of the PSL are Chinese operatives'; Part III: 'Americans have genuine concerns... These concerns are authentic and need to be heard,' with a foreign campaign running 'parallel to this domestic, democratic movement.' Genuine drivers (electricity prices, water use, noise, land, tax abatements) are real and widespread. The warranted findings are narrower and stronger for it: (i) a real, CCP-aligned Singham funding network exists; (ii) its affiliated activists genuinely participated in specific data-center fights; (iii) whether that participation DIRECTED (vs merely joined/amplified) the broader movement is unproven, and the aggregate-impact figure is unsubstantiated.
the_honest_reading: There is a documented, CCP-aligned influence network around Neville Roy Singham (NYT 2023; IRS 990s ~$278M into six US nonprofits; Delhi Police 'ultimate paymaster' finding 2024; Rubio FARA referral; a reported DOJ grand-jury probe), and activists from that ecosystem (PSL and allied groups) genuinely took part in ~21 US data-center-opposition campaigns, several of which produced moratoria, a ban, and project withdrawals. That much is fact. The Bitcoin Policy Institute's three-part series then makes a bigger, graded claim — that this constitutes a coordinated FOREIGN INFLUENCE CAMPAIGN against American AI that blocked '$23.6B' of infrastructure — which the reports themselves hedge (PSL's role 'ranged from lead organizer to one member of a broader coalition'; 'not asserting PSL members are Chinese operatives'; genuine local concerns are 'authentic'), whose aggregate dollar figure has no stated methodology, and which is authored by a pro-AI/pro-Bitcoin institute with an interest in the framing. Read by the composition guard, the finding is: a real funding network + real participation + parallel messaging — NOT proof that foreign direction originated or controls the opposition. Read by read-words-for-intent, Singham's network conspicuously does not disclose its financing (PSL is exempt from public reporting) and echoes CCP positions, which cuts toward alignment; but non-disclosure + alignment is not the same as command. Both the network facts and the report's hedges are retained, with dates. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: ~$278M from Singham-controlled entities into six US nonprofits 2017-23 (IRS 990s per Fox Mar 2026; NYT Aug 2023) — People's Forum/ANSWER/BreakThrough/Tricontinental/Justice & Education Fund/CodePink (spouse Jodie Evans)

US citizen in Shanghai, ex-Huawei consultant, CCP-workshop ties (NYT 2023); Delhi Police chargesheet named Chinese govt 'ultimate paymaster' (May 2024); Rubio FARA referral (2023); reported DOJ grand-jury probe; Singham denies taking orders from any party/government
edges.note: OVERLAY: the funder (network facts documented; denial noted)

edges.raw_instrument: PSL leadership overlaps the nonprofits; PSL/allied groups ran or joined ~21 data-center-opposition campaigns in 14 states -> ~10 moratoria + Monterey Park ban + Google(IN)/QTS-Blackstone(WI) withdrawals; report concedes PSL role 'ranged from lead organizer to one member of a broader coalition'
edges.note: OVERLAY: participation documented; DIRECTION of the movement is the graded inference

edges.raw_instrument: Chinese state media (CGTN/China Daily/Global Times) ran attributed anti-US-data-center items; content alignment with CCP positions (NYT) — 'directed campaign against American AI' is the report's inference, not shown

2025-26 inquiries: House Energy & Commerce (Jun 4 2026), Sen. Cotton (Jun 10 2026), Ways & Means letters to Tricontinental (Feb 9 / May 5 2026), House Oversight (Sep 15 2025), Senate Judiciary (spring 2025); DOJ FARA scrutiny

publisher of the 3-part series (Sam Lyman, May-Jun 2026) — a pro-Bitcoin/pro-AI-infrastructure 501(c)(3) with an interest in the framing; $23.6B aggregate has no stated methodology (~$17B of named projects)
edges.note: OVERLAY: source-interest flag (read words-for-intent on the framer)

edges.raw_instrument: the contested object: AI data-center buildout is the target of the opposition campaigns the series tracks (genuine local drivers — power/water/noise/land/tax abatements — run parallel to the alleged foreign amplification)
edges.note: OVERLAY: ties to the AI-datacenter buildout thread
